area: Plane Cycles — sprints/phases, dates, active cycle, transfer, burndown, REST API, and mapping ASCP's 7 phases / 49 waves onto cycles
summary: Cycles are Plane's time-boxed sprints: one active cycle per project by default (the current date falls inside its start/due dates), with upcoming and completed states. They carry a burn-down/build-up chart, a progress percentage, and assignee/label/state distributions, and they support one-click transfer of incomplete items to the next cycle. A work item can belong to only ONE cycle at a time — this is the central constraint that decides how you map phases/waves. The REST API (identical paths on cloud and self-hosted, only the base host differs) fully supports create/list/update/delete cycles, bulk-add work items via an `issues` array, transfer incomplete items via `new_cycle_id`, and archive — all callable for ASCP. For the ASCP board, the clean, Tier-1 mapping is: 7 cycles = the 7 platform phases (Phase 0–6) as sequential sprints (one active at a time), since phases are mutually exclusive timeboxes and a task lives in exactly one phase. The 49 dependency-graph waves are NOT a good fit for cycles (49 cycles is unusable, and waves are a dependency ordering, not a calendar) — represent waves with a `wave-NN` label or a custom property and use cycle burndown for phase-level rhythm. Epics→story→task→subtask hierarchy is orthogonal to cycles and uses Epics + parent/sub-issue, while the 8 ASCP epics could alternatively be Modules. NOTE: cycle progress charts require Pro, auto-schedule/recurring cycles require Business, and parallel (overlapping) cycles require Enterprise — on a free self-hosted instance you create cycles sequentially via API and transfer manually.
key_findings: CYCLE = SPRINT/PHASE. A cycle is Plane's timebox (Agile sprint/iteration). Lifecycle states: Upcoming (start_date in future), Active/current (today is within start–due), Completed (due date passed; not editable). By default two cycles cannot have overlapping dates, so without the Enterprise 'Parallel cycles' feature you get exactly ONE active cycle per project at a time — which maps perfectly to 'one phase in flight'. Source: docs.plane.so/core-concepts/cycles.

ONE-CYCLE-PER-ITEM CONSTRAINT (decisive for mapping). 'A work item can only belong to one cycle at a time' — even with parallel cycles enabled. This means you cannot put the same task in both a 'Phase' cycle and a 'Wave' cycle. You must pick cycles for ONE axis (phases) and represent the other axis (waves) with labels/properties.

CREATE A CYCLE (REST): POST {base}/api/v1/workspaces/{workspace_slug}/projects/{project_id}/cycles/ — body: name (required, string), description, start_date, end_date (ISO-8601 e.g. '2026-06-16T00:00:00Z'), owned_by (defaults to caller), timezone (IANA), external_id, external_source. Header: X-API-Key: <token> (or Authorization: Bearer for OAuth). Scope: projects.cycles:write. Returns 201 with id, status ('current'/'upcoming'/'completed'), and rollup counts total_issues/completed_issues/started_issues/unstarted_issues/backlog_issues/cancelled_issues.

ADD WORK ITEMS TO A CYCLE (bulk): POST {base}/api/v1/workspaces/{workspace_slug}/projects/{project_id}/cycles/{cycle_id}/cycle-issues/ — body uses an ARRAY field named 'issues': {"issues":["<uuid>","<uuid>",...]}. Scope projects.cycles:write. This is the call you use to bulk-assign all tasks of a phase to its cycle. List: GET .../cycle-issues/. Remove one: DELETE .../cycle-issues/{issue_id}/.

TRANSFER INCOMPLETE ITEMS (carryover): POST {base}/api/v1/workspaces/{workspace_slug}/projects/{project_id}/cycles/{cycle_id}/transfer-issues/ — body: {"new_cycle_id":"<dest-cycle-uuid>"}. Per docs it 'captures a progress snapshot and transfers only unfinished work items' to the destination (active or upcoming) cycle. This is the API behind the UI 'Transfer incomplete work to the next cycle in one click'. Completed cycles are read-only except for this transfer.

LIST / FILTER CYCLES: GET .../cycles/ with query params cursor, cycle_view (filter by status — values like current, upcoming, completed, draft), order_by, fields, expand, per_page (default 20, max 100). There is NO dedicated 'active cycle' endpoint — to find the active one, list and filter by cycle_view=current (or check status=='current'). Other ops: PATCH .../cycles/{id}/ (update dates/name), DELETE .../cycles/{id}/, POST .../cycles/{id}/archive/ + /unarchive/, GET .../cycles/archived/.

BURN-DOWN / PROGRESS (Pro tier). Each cycle has a burn-down chart: Scope (total work items planned) vs 'Today's ideal pending' vs actual Pending; 'Leading' (ahead, green) / 'Trailing' (behind, red). A Build-up chart tracks completed vs 'today's ideal done'. The cycle summary shows overall progress %, completed/pending counts, days remaining, and distribution by assignee/label/state. The API response already returns the raw counts (total/completed/started/unstarted/backlog) to compute progress without Pro.

TIER GATING (matters for self-hosted ASCP). Cycle progress charts/burndown = Pro. Auto-schedule + Recurring cycles (auto-create N future cycles, duration in weeks, cooldown days, auto-rollover of incomplete items) = Business; configured at Project Settings > Features > Cycles (fields: Cycle Title, Cycle Duration, Cooldown Period, Cycle starts day, Number of future cycles, Auto-rollover work items). Parallel (overlapping) cycles = Enterprise Grid. v1.16.0 also added Milestones — project-level checkpoints that group cycles+modules+work items under a deadline (e.g. 'Phase 6 complete by ...'). On free self-hosted you still get core cycles + create/transfer via API; you just script the sequencing and read counts from the API instead of the chart.

SELF-HOSTED API: identical endpoint paths and X-API-Key auth as cloud; only the base host differs (http(s)://<your-plane-host>/api/v1/... instead of https://api.plane.so/api/v1/...). Generate the token at Profile Settings > Personal Access Tokens > Add personal access token. Rate limit 60 req/min per key (watch X-RateLimit-Remaining / X-RateLimit-Reset) — batch the cycle-issues add (one call per phase with the full issues[] array) to stay under it.

CYCLES vs MODULES vs EPICS (so you don't double-map ASCP's structure). Cycles = time/sprint axis (the 7 phases). Modules = grouping by component/theme/feature, Epic-like, and an item CAN be in a module and a cycle simultaneously (modules are a separate axis from cycles). Epics = the true parent layer for epic→story→task→subtask (set via the work item's Parent property; sub-issues for tasks). So ASCP's 8 epics → Plane Epics (hierarchy) and/or Modules (grouping), while the 7 phases → Cycles. Waves are neither — they're a dependency ordering.

recommendations.title: Map the 7 platform phases (Phase 0–6) to 7 sequential Cycles — NOT the 49 waves
recommendations.detail: Phases are mutually-exclusive calendar timeboxes and every task belongs to exactly one phase, which is precisely the cycle data model (one item → one cycle, one active cycle at a time). 49 waves would mean 49 cycles (unusable in the Cycles UI, no single 'active' meaning) and waves are a dependency DAG ordering, not dates — they don't belong on a calendar axis. Keep cycles = phases for a clean, navigable, one-active-sprint board.
recommendations.how: Create 7 cycles named 'Phase 0 — Foundation' … 'Phase 6 — Predictive Routing' (use your real phase titles). Per cycle: POST {base}/api/v1/workspaces/{slug}/projects/{ascp_project_id}/cycles/ with body {"name":"Phase 2 — ...","start_date":"2026-06-16T00:00:00Z","end_date":"2026-06-30T00:00:00Z"}. Give them contiguous, non-overlapping date ranges so exactly one is 'current' at a time. Capture each returned cycle id. Then bulk-assign that phase's tasks: POST .../cycles/{cycle_id}/cycle-issues/ with {"issues":[<all task uuids in that phase>]}.

recommendations.title: Represent the 49 dependency-graph waves as a `wave-NN` label (or custom property), not cycles
recommendations.detail: Waves are an execution-order/parallelism dimension within a phase. A label keeps wave membership visible, groupable, and filterable on the board (group-by Label, or filter wave-03) WITHOUT consuming the single cycle slot each task has — leaving the cycle free for the phase. This preserves maximal traceability (phase via cycle + wave via label) with zero conflict.
recommendations.how: Create labels wave-01 … wave-49 (POST .../projects/{id}/labels/ with {"name":"wave-07","color":"#6366F1"}). On each task set labels via the issue API (PATCH .../work-items/{id}/ with {"labels":[<wave_label_id>, ...]}). In the UI, on the Cycle/board view use Group by → Labels or Filter → Labels = wave-07 to see a single wave inside its phase. Optionally add a 'Wave' number custom property for sorting if you have the custom-properties feature.

recommendations.title: Use the active-cycle + burndown as the live phase tracker; drive carryover via the transfer API
recommendations.detail: Because only one cycle is 'current', the active cycle IS the in-flight phase. Its burndown (Scope vs ideal-pending, Leading/Trailing) gives you the phase observability you want. When a phase's end_date passes with unfinished tasks, transfer them into the next phase's cycle in one operation so metrics and carryover history stay accurate (completed cycles are otherwise read-only).
recommendations.how: To find the active phase: GET .../cycles/?cycle_view=current. At phase boundary, carry over incomplete tasks: POST .../cycles/{ending_cycle_id}/transfer-issues/ with {"new_cycle_id":"{next_phase_cycle_id}"} — it snapshots progress and moves only unfinished items. For a free self-hosted instance, compute progress yourself from the cycle object counts (completed_issues / total_issues) returned by GET .../cycles/{id}/ rather than relying on the Pro chart.

recommendations.title: Keep the epic→story→task→subtask hierarchy on the Epic/Parent axis, and consider Modules for the 8 ASCP epics
recommendations.detail: Cycles must not be overloaded with hierarchy. Plane Epics provide the parent layer (epic spans multiple cycles), the 49 EARS stories sit under epics, the 186 tasks under stories, and subtasks as sub-issues. Because Modules are a SEPARATE axis from cycles, an item can be in both a Module (its epic/feature group) and a Cycle (its phase) at once — giving you full epic-and-phase traceability simultaneously.
recommendations.how: Model the 8 epics as Plane Epics (enable Epics in Project Settings > Features) and set each story's/task's Parent to its epic/story. If you also want flat grouping + per-epic progress bars, create 8 Modules (POST .../projects/{id}/modules/) and add the epic's tasks to its module (POST .../modules/{module_id}/module-issues/ with {"issues":[...]}) — independent of the phase cycle each task is in. On the board, Group by Cycle for the phase view and Group by Module for the epic view.

recommendations.title: If/when on Business+ self-hosted, switch to Recurring/Auto-schedule cycles for hands-off phase rotation; add Milestones for phase gates
recommendations.detail: Manual cycle creation works on free, but Business adds Recurring cycles that auto-create the next N timeboxes and auto-rollover incomplete items — removing the scripted transfer step. Milestones (v1.16.0) let you set a deadline checkpoint (e.g. 'Phases 0–3 GA') that aggregates the relevant cycles/modules/work items, which is ideal for the enterprise 'gate' between phase clusters.
recommendations.how: Project Settings > Features > Cycles: set Cycle Title (e.g. 'Phase'), Cycle Duration (weeks), Cooldown Period (days between phases), Cycle starts day, Number of future cycles, and toggle Auto-rollover work items. For gates, create a Milestone (Project level) and attach the cycles/modules that must finish by the gate date. Reserve Parallel cycles (Enterprise) ONLY if you ever need two phases genuinely running at once — otherwise keep one active cycle for clarity.

area: Plane Modules — feature groupings, lead/members, progress, REST API, and mapping the 8 ASCP epics onto modules for feature-level rollups
summary: Modules in Plane are persistent, feature-level groupings of work items (a "feature," a microservice, a release theme) — orthogonal to Cycles, which are time-boxed sprints. A work item can belong to one or more modules AND a cycle at the same time, so Modules give you the feature-level rollup (epic completion %) while Cycles give you the sprint-level rollup. Each module carries a name, description, an optional single lead (UUID), an optional members array (UUIDs, independent of work-item assignees), start_date/target_date, and a status enum (backlog/planned/in-progress/paused/completed/cancelled). Progress is auto-computed from completed vs total work items and surfaced as per-module counts (total/completed/started/unstarted/backlog/cancelled) plus a module-pipeline summary, a progress scatter plot, and (rolling out) Burn-down/Build-up progress charts. The full module lifecycle is available over the public REST API (create, update, archive, add/list/remove work items) — unlike Views and Pages which are web-UI only. For the ASCP board, map the 8 epics 1:1 onto 8 modules (epic = module, the feature-level container) so each epic gets a live completion rollup independent of the 49 dependency waves you express as Cycles. Critical API gotcha: module membership is NOT settable via the work-item PATCH body — it is managed only through the dedicated POST /modules/{id}/module-issues/ endpoint with an {"issues":[...]} array.
key_findings: MODEL: Modules = structural/feature grouping (persistent); Cycles = time-boxed sprints (transient). They are orthogonal and composable — a single work item can sit in a Module AND a Cycle simultaneously, and can belong to MULTIPLE modules at once (e.g. a 'Feature' module + a 'Release' module). This is exactly the two-axis rollup the ASCP board needs: 8 epics->8 Modules (feature axis) x 49 waves->Cycles (time axis). Source: docs.plane.so/core-concepts/modules.

CREATE MODULE (REST): POST /api/v1/workspaces/{workspace_slug}/projects/{project_id}/modules/ with header X-API-Key: $PLANE_API_KEY (or Authorization: Bearer for OAuth). Body fields: name (string, REQUIRED), description (string), start_date ('YYYY-MM-DD'), target_date ('YYYY-MM-DD'), status (enum), lead (UUID of a member), members (array of member UUIDs), external_id + external_source (for idempotent sync from .kiro). Required OAuth scope: projects.modules:write. Returns 201 with id + rollup counts (total_issues, completed_issues, started_issues, unstarted_issues, backlog_issues, cancelled_issues).

STATUS ENUM (exact values): backlog, planned, in-progress, paused, completed, cancelled. Note the hyphen in 'in-progress'. 'backlog' is the default for a new module; 'completed' is the state when all work items are Done. These are module-level lifecycle states, distinct from the 12 custom agent-lifecycle WORK-ITEM states on the ASCP board.

ADD WORK ITEMS TO MODULE (REST): POST /api/v1/workspaces/{workspace_slug}/projects/{project_id}/modules/{module_id}/module-issues/ — note the path segment is 'module-issues' (NOT 'issues'). Body is exactly {"issues": ["<uuid>", "<uuid>", ...]} — the array field name is 'issues' and it accepts multiple work-item UUIDs in one call (bulk add). Returns 200 with an array of module-issue link objects (id, module, issue, sub_issues_count). Required scope projects.modules:write.

LIST module work items: GET .../modules/{module_id}/module-issues/ (paginated: cursor, per_page default 20 / max 100, expand=assignees, fields=...). REMOVE a work item: DELETE .../modules/{module_id}/module-issues/{issue_id}/. ARCHIVE/UNARCHIVE module: POST .../modules/{module_id}/archive/ and .../unarchive/. Full CRUD: GET/PATCH/DELETE on .../modules/{module_id}/.

CRITICAL API GOTCHA — modules are NOT in the work-item body: the work-item PATCH endpoint (PATCH .../issues/{issue_id}/) only accepts assignees[], labels[], state, parent (and core fields). There is NO 'modules' or 'module' field on the work-item create/update body over the public v1 API. To put a work item in a module you MUST call the module-issues POST endpoint. Therefore multi-module membership = one module-issues POST per module (the UI does this transparently; the API does not).

LEAD vs MEMBERS semantics: 'lead' is a SINGLE UUID (one owner per module — map to the epic owner/responsible agent-role). 'members' is an ARRAY of UUIDs and is INDEPENDENT of work-item assignees — you can add stakeholders/reviewers to a module who aren't assigned any of its tasks. Both are settable on create and changeable via PATCH .../modules/{module_id}/. (Plane shipped a 2025 fix specifically for adding leads/members during module creation, so set them at create time.)

PROGRESS / OBSERVABILITY: progress is auto-calculated = completed work items / total work items; no manual updating. Surfaced as: (1) per-module rollup counts on every module object via API; (2) a Modules-tab 'module pipeline' summary (how many completed / in-progress / planned / paused); (3) a module progress SCATTER PLOT (one dot per module to spot stalled vs on-track); (4) Burn-down / Build-up Progress Charts — launched for Cycles first, explicitly rolling out to Modules + Home + Dashboards + Analytics. This is the feature-level observability layer the Tier-1 board wants.

UI LAYOUTS & creation: press 'M' anywhere in a project, or Modules page -> 'Add Module'. Module list page offers List, Gallery (cards with per-state work-item count breakdown), and Timeline (Gantt-style) layouts; inside a module you get the standard work-item layouts + an info/details panel (lead, members, dates, progress, properties). Modules can be Favorited, Exported (all work items + details), Archived, and Searched/Sorted/Filtered by name/state/due date.

HIERARCHY note for epic->story->task->subtask: Plane expresses parent/child via the work-item 'parent' field (PATCH parent=<uuid>), giving you sub-issues. Modules are a flat GROUPING layer on top of that tree — they do NOT replace the parent hierarchy. So the clean ASCP design is: parent field builds the epic/story/task/subtask tree; Modules give the 8 epic-level rollups; Cycles give the 49-wave time-boxing. add-module-issue returns sub_issues_count so a module rollup is aware of nested sub-issues.

API SURFACE caveat for the board plan: Modules + Cycles are fully REST-driveable (good — they can be provisioned programmatically from the .kiro spec). Views and Pages remain web-UI only with no public API, so the feature-rollup architecture should lean on Modules/Cycles for anything that must be automated/reproducible, and reserve Views/Pages for hand-curated human-facing surfaces.

recommendations.title: Map the 8 epics 1:1 onto 8 Modules (epic = module = feature-rollup container)
recommendations.detail: Create exactly one module per ASCP epic so each epic gets a live, auto-computed completion percentage and per-state counts independent of the 49 dependency waves. Name modules to mirror the .kiro phase/epic taxonomy (e.g. 'EPIC-0 Spec Compilation', 'EPIC-3 Agent Execution') so the Modules tab reads as a feature map with zero context. Set status at create time: epics not yet started = 'planned' (or 'backlog'), the active phase = 'in-progress'. This gives the feature-level rollup; the 12 custom work-item states still drive the agent lifecycle underneath.
recommendations.how: For each epic: POST /api/v1/workspaces/{slug}/projects/{project_id}/modules/ with X-API-Key, body {"name":"EPIC-3 Agent Execution","description":"<EARS summary>","status":"planned","start_date":"2026-06-16","target_date":"2026-08-01","lead":"<owner-uuid>","members":["<reviewer-uuid>"],"external_id":"epic-3","external_source":"kiro-spec"}. Capture the returned id as the module_id for the next step.

recommendations.title: Attach every story+task+subtask to its epic's module via bulk module-issues POST
recommendations.detail: After creating work items, link all 243 items to their owning epic's module so the rollup counts are populated. Do it in bulk per module (the endpoint accepts an array) rather than per item. Remember this CANNOT be done in the work-item create/PATCH body — it is a separate call.
recommendations.how: POST /api/v1/workspaces/{slug}/projects/{project_id}/modules/{module_id}/module-issues/ with body {"issues":["<uuid1>","<uuid2>",...]} (all work-item UUIDs belonging to that epic). One call per module; batch the UUIDs. Verify with GET .../modules/{module_id}/module-issues/?per_page=100 and reconcile total_issues against your 243-item inventory.

recommendations.title: Use Modules for the FEATURE axis and Cycles for the TIME axis — assign items to both
recommendations.detail: Because a work item can live in a module and a cycle simultaneously, model the 49 dependency-graph waves as Cycles (sprint/time-box rollups) and the 8 epics as Modules (feature rollups). A task in Wave 12 that implements Epic 3 gets added to both Cycle-12 and Module-EPIC-3. This yields two independent, automatically-maintained rollups from one set of work items — the dual traceability a Tier-1 board needs.
recommendations.how: Add to module via the module-issues POST above; add the same UUIDs to the cycle via POST /api/v1/workspaces/{slug}/projects/{project_id}/cycles/{cycle_id}/cycle-issues/ with body {"issues":[...]}. Drive both from the .kiro dependency graph: wave->cycle membership, epic->module membership.

recommendations.title: Set a deterministic lead + members on every module at create time
recommendations.detail: Assign each epic-module a single 'lead' (the responsible agent-role/owner) and a 'members' array of reviewers/stakeholders so the Modules detail panel answers 'who owns this feature' with zero onboarding context. Members are independent of task assignees, so you can surface human reviewers and the orchestrator even when they aren't assigned individual tasks. Set these on create (Plane had a 2025 bug adding lead/members post-hoc, since fixed, but create-time is safest).
recommendations.how: Include "lead":"<uuid>" (single) and "members":["<uuid>",...] in the create-module body. To change later: PATCH /api/v1/workspaces/{slug}/projects/{project_id}/modules/{module_id}/ with {"lead":"<uuid>","members":[...]}. Resolve member UUIDs from GET /api/v1/workspaces/{slug}/members/.

recommendations.title: Lean on module rollup fields + Gallery/Timeline layouts + Progress Charts for observability
recommendations.detail: Expose the auto-computed rollups as the board's feature-level observability layer: the module object's completed_issues/total_issues drive a live completion %, the Modules-tab pipeline summary + scatter plot show which epics are stalled vs on-track at a glance, and the Burn-down/Build-up Progress Charts (now extending from Cycles to Modules) give trend lines. Set the Modules page to Timeline layout for the phase-Gantt and Gallery for the at-a-glance per-state card breakdown.
recommendations.how: Read rollups via GET .../modules/{module_id}/ (fields total_issues, completed_issues, started_issues, unstarted_issues, backlog_issues, cancelled_issues) — compute % = completed_issues/total_issues. In UI: Project -> Modules tab -> layout switcher -> Timeline (Gantt) or Gallery (cards); open a module -> info icon for the details/progress panel. No manual progress entry — it tracks Done-state work items automatically.

recommendations.title: Provision modules idempotently with external_id/external_source so re-runs don't duplicate
recommendations.detail: Because the whole module lifecycle is REST-driveable (unlike Views/Pages), generate the 8 modules from the .kiro spec in a reproducible script. Stamp external_id (e.g. the epic key) and external_source ('kiro-spec') on each so a re-provision can detect-and-update instead of creating duplicates — essential for a maximal-traceability, reproducible board.
recommendations.how: On create, set "external_id":"epic-3","external_source":"kiro-spec". On re-run, GET .../modules/?per_page=100, match on external_id, and PATCH existing modules instead of POSTing new ones. Keep a kiro-epic-key -> module_id map in your provisioning state file so module-issues links stay stable across runs.

area: Plane Saved Views + Display Options — curated VIEW SET for the ASCP agentic SDLC board
summary: Plane Views are web-UI-only saved configurations (NO public REST API for views or pages-reliably; only cycles/modules/work-items/labels/states are REST-creatable). A View persists exactly four things: layout, filters, display options (group-by + sub-group-by + order-by + card display-properties), and sort. The exact vocabulary from the official docs (docs.plane.so/core-concepts/issues/display-options): Group by AND Sub-group by share the SAME option set — {State, Priority, Cycle, Module, Labels, Assignees, Created By, Milestones, Releases, Work Item Types, None}; Order by = {Manual, Last Created, Last Updated, Start Date, Due Date, Priority}; Display properties (card toggles) = {ID, Work item Type, Assignee, Start Date, Due Date, Labels, Priority, State, Sub-work item Count, Attachment Count, Link, Estimate, Modules, Cycle}; plus two toggles {Show sub-work items, Show empty groups}. Sub-grouping (swimlanes) works ONLY in the Board (Kanban) layout — confirmed by Plane's own marketing ("unlock swimlanes in three clicks") and docs. Five layouts exist: List, Board, Calendar, Table (spreadsheet), Timeline (Gantt); only Board supports the two-dimensional group×sub-group swimlane matrix. Because views are web-only, the ASCP board needs all six curated views created manually in the UI (or seeded via UI), while the underlying work-item structure (states, labels, cycles, modules, epic→story→task hierarchy) is API-provisionable. I deliver six concrete view recipes with exact group-by / sub-group / order-by / filters / display-property sets tuned to ASCP's 12 agent-lifecycle states, 22 labels, agent-role labels, and 7-phase/cycle structure.
key_findings: VIEWS HAVE NO PUBLIC REST API. Confirmed against developers.plane.so: the 180+ REST endpoints cover work items, cycles, modules, labels, states, and intake — but NOT views. Pages technically have endpoints yet are unreliable on self-hosted (GitHub issue #8986: pages endpoints unreachable via /api/v1/ with API-key auth on self-hosted). PRACTICAL CONSEQUENCE: every Saved View in the ASCP curated set must be created by hand in the web UI (Project → Views → Add view, or apply filters then 'Save View'). Plan the seeding as a one-time manual UI pass; only the data layer (states/labels/cycles/modules/hierarchy) is scriptable via api.plane.so/api/v1/workspaces/{slug}/projects/{id}/...

EXACT GROUP-BY / SUB-GROUP-BY OPTION SET (identical for both, per docs.plane.so/core-concepts/issues/display-options): State, Priority, Cycle, Module, Labels, Assignees, Created By, Milestones, Releases, Work Item Types, None. NOTE: you cannot group by 'parent/epic' directly — epic→story→task hierarchy is expressed via the parent link + 'Show sub-work items' toggle and via Modules, not via a group-by axis. To get an epic swimlane, model each of the 8 epics as a Module and group/sub-group by Module.

EXACT ORDER-BY SET: Manual, Last Created, Last Updated, Start Date, Due Date, Priority. 'Manual' = drag-to-rank (the only way to impose an arbitrary pipeline/wave ordering). There is NO 'group order by state-sequence' — board column order follows the State definition order, so the 12 ASCP states must be created in pipeline sequence (Backlog → Agent-Triaged → Spec-Compiling → Spec-Verified → Plan-Approved → Agent-Executing → In-Verification → Human-Review → Done, with HANDOFF/Blocked/Failed) so the Kanban columns read left-to-right correctly.

EXACT DISPLAY-PROPERTY (card) TOGGLES: ID, Work item Type, Assignee, Start Date, Due Date, Labels, Priority, State, Sub-work item Count, Attachment Count, Link, Estimate, Modules, Cycle. For a zero-context, observability-grade card, enable: ID + State + Priority + Assignee + Labels + Cycle + Module + Sub-work item Count + Due Date. Disable Start Date/Estimate/Attachment/Link on the kanban to keep cards compact; enable the full set only on the Table layout.

SWIMLANES = SUB-GROUP, BOARD-LAYOUT-ONLY. Sub-group-by produces horizontal swimlanes and is supported ONLY in the Board (Kanban) layout (List/Table/Calendar/Timeline do not sub-group). The canonical two-dimensional pattern Plane itself recommends is Group by State × Sub-group by Assignees ('state of tasks per team member'). For agentic boards substitute Assignees→Labels(agent-role) or Module(epic). 'Show empty groups' ON keeps all 12 state columns visible even when empty (critical for pipeline legibility); turn it OFF on filtered triage views to reduce noise.

LAYOUT MATRIX: List (grouped ordered list, drag-rank, shows parent-child), Board (Kanban columns = group-by, swimlanes = sub-group, the only 2-D layout), Calendar (due-date placement, drag to reschedule), Table (spreadsheet — all columns, bulk edit, keyboard nav — best for evidence/audit dumps), Timeline (Gantt — start/target dates, dependencies, critical path — best for the 49-wave dependency graph). Workspace-level views support ONLY the spreadsheet/Table layout; all five layouts are available only inside a project view.

VIEW PERSISTENCE + SHARING: A View saves {layout, filters, display options (group/sub-group/order + card props), sort}. Access levels: Public (default, all project members), Private (Pro — owner-only), Published (public web link, no account needed, publisher can restrict layouts and toggle comments/reactions/votes). For an AI-Labs-grade shareable board, Publish the Pipeline view read-only for stakeholder zero-context onboarding.

FILTER VOCABULARY: State, State group, Priority, Assignee, Created by, Labels, Start date (range), Due date (range), Cycle, Module, plus custom properties. Filters AND across fields and OR within a field's multi-select. Blocked/handoff views are built by filtering State to the {HANDOFF, Blocked, Failed} subset; evidence-pending by filtering State=In-Verification (optionally Label='evidence-required').

recommendations.title: View 1 — PIPELINE (the default board): Board / Group-by State / Sub-group-by Module / Order Priority
recommendations.detail: The flagship zero-context view. Kanban columns are the 12 agent-lifecycle states in sequence (Backlog→Agent-Triaged→Spec-Compiling→Spec-Verified→Plan-Approved→Agent-Executing→In-Verification→Human-Review→Done, plus HANDOFF/Blocked/Failed). Horizontal swimlanes = Module (one lane per epic, since the 8 epics are modeled as Modules). 'Show empty groups' ON so all 12 stages always render; 'Show sub-work items' ON so story→task nesting is visible. This is the single screen that tells anyone, with no prior context, exactly where every one of the 243 work items sits in the agent lifecycle and which epic owns it.
recommendations.how: Project → Views → Add view → name 'Pipeline'. Layout=Board. Display options: Group by=State; Sub-group by=Module; Order by=Priority; toggles Show empty groups=ON, Show sub-work items=ON. Display properties: ID, State, Priority, Assignee, Labels, Cycle, Sub-work item Count, Due Date. No filter (show all). Then Publish read-only for stakeholder onboarding. PREREQ via REST: create the 12 states in pipeline order (POST /workspaces/{slug}/projects/{id}/states/) and the 8 epics as Modules (POST .../modules/) before building the view.

recommendations.title: View 2 — BY-EPIC roadmap: Board / Group-by Module / Sub-group-by State / Order Manual
recommendations.detail: Inverts View 1 to give an epic-first roadmap. Columns = the 8 epics (Modules); swimlanes = state. Lets a reviewer see, per epic, how its stories/tasks are distributed across the lifecycle — instantly surfacing epics stuck pre-Spec-Verified vs epics nearing Done. Order-by Manual lets you hand-rank epics by delivery priority. Pair with the Timeline layout variant (same group-by Module) for the dependency/critical-path read across the 49 waves.
recommendations.how: Add view 'By Epic'. Layout=Board. Group by=Module; Sub-group by=State; Order by=Manual; Show empty groups=ON. Display properties: ID, State, Priority, Assignee, Sub-work item Count, Cycle. For the Gantt companion: clone as 'By Epic — Timeline', Layout=Timeline, Group by=Module (sub-group not available in Timeline), ensure Start Date/Due Date set on items so the 49 waves render as bars with dependencies.

recommendations.title: View 3 — BY-AGENT-ROLE workload: Board / Group-by Labels / Sub-group-by State / filter Label∈{agent-role}
recommendations.detail: Operational view of which agent role owns what. Columns = the agent-role labels (e.g. triage-agent, spec-agent, plan-agent, exec-agent, verify-agent, review-agent — drawn from your 22-label set); swimlanes = state. This is the agentic analogue of Plane's recommended 'group by State × sub-group by Assignees' workload pattern — substitute Labels for Assignees because work is owned by agent ROLE not a human assignee. Reveals over/under-loaded roles and where each role's queue is backing up in the lifecycle.
recommendations.how: Add view 'By Agent Role'. Layout=Board. Group by=Labels; Sub-group by=State; Order by=Priority. Filter: Labels = (multi-select the agent-role label subset only). Show empty groups=OFF (hide non-role labels). Display properties: ID, State, Priority, Assignee, Cycle, Module, Due Date. Note: grouping by Labels creates one column per label and a multi-label item appears in multiple columns — keep the role labels mutually exclusive (one role label per work item) for a clean read.

recommendations.title: View 4 — BY-PHASE / SPRINT: List or Board / Group-by Cycle / Sub-group-by State / Order Priority
recommendations.detail: Maps the 7 Spec-to-Evidence phases (0–6) to Plane Cycles (one Cycle per phase, time-boxed as sprints). Group-by Cycle gives a phase-by-phase burndown read; sub-group by State (Board) or just group by Cycle (List) shows progress within each phase. Because Cycles carry start/end dates and burndown charts natively, this is the sprint-velocity and phase-completion view. Use List layout for the dense planning read, Board for the active-sprint standup.
recommendations.how: Add view 'By Phase'. PREREQ via REST: create 7 Cycles (POST .../cycles/ with start_date/end_date) named Phase 0…Phase 6 and assign work items (POST .../cycles/{cycle_id}/cycle-issues/). View: Layout=List (or Board); Group by=Cycle; (Board only) Sub-group by=State; Order by=Priority; Show empty groups=ON. Display properties: ID, State, Priority, Assignee, Labels, Module, Sub-work item Count, Due Date. Optionally add a filter Cycle=current-phase for an active-sprint variant.

recommendations.title: View 5 — BLOCKED / HANDOFF triage: Board / Group-by State / filter State∈{HANDOFF,Blocked,Failed} / Order Last Updated
recommendations.detail: The exception-handling cockpit. Filters the board down to only the three off-happy-path states so blockers and agent→human handoffs never get buried. Columns = the 3 states; order by Last Updated surfaces the stalest blockers at a glance. This is the view a human operator lives in — it is the single place where the lifecycle's escape hatches (HANDOFF for human input, Blocked for dependencies, Failed for verification failures) are all visible together.
recommendations.how: Add view 'Blocked / Handoff'. Layout=Board. Group by=State; Order by=Last Updated; Show empty groups=ON (so an empty board visibly means 'nothing blocked'). Filter: State = HANDOFF, Blocked, Failed (multi-select). Display properties: ID, State, Priority, Assignee, Labels, Module, Cycle, Link, Updated date. Add Priority=Urgent/High as a secondary filter for a 'critical blockers' variant. Publish read-only so stakeholders can watch the blocker queue.

recommendations.title: View 6 — EVIDENCE-PENDING / verification queue: Table / filter State=In-Verification / Order Last Updated + full display props
recommendations.detail: The audit/traceability view, in Table (spreadsheet) layout for maximal column density and bulk action. Filters to State=In-Verification (the gate where agent output awaits evidence/proof) — optionally AND Label='evidence-required'. Table layout exposes every property as a column (Estimate, Attachment Count, Link, dates) so a reviewer can scan evidence completeness across the whole queue and bulk-advance items to Human-Review or Done. Best layout for the proof-of-work / observability mandate.
recommendations.how: Add view 'Evidence Pending'. Layout=Table. Group by=Module (Table supports single-level grouping); Order by=Last Updated. Filter: State=In-Verification (AND Labels='evidence-required' if you maintain that label). Display properties: enable the FULL set — ID, Work item Type, State, Priority, Assignee, Labels, Cycle, Modules, Estimate, Start Date, Due Date, Sub-work item Count, Attachment Count, Link. Use bulk-edit (Table multi-row select) to move verified items onward. GLOBAL NOTE: all six views are web-UI-only — script the states/labels/cycles/modules/hierarchy via REST first, then create these six views by hand once; there is no API to seed them.

recommendations.title: Set Identifier ASCP and keep the description to one sentence
recommendations.detail: Identifier is 12 uppercase chars max with no specials hyphen and period blocked yields items ASCP 1 to ASCP 243
recommendations.how: Project Settings General tab Identifier ASCP one line description set icon and cover image

recommendations.title: Author one Start Here Page in the web UI then Lock it
recommendations.detail: No public Pages API on self hosted use a mission first 7 section layout mission legend read board state machine traceability cycles links
recommendations.how: Press D then slash Callout and an H1 per section use the auto Table of Contents then page options Lock Page

recommendations.title: Render the 12 states and the traceability then Publish
recommendations.detail: Table for the state legend Draw io for the state machine at mention an epic story task Tables for the 22 labels and Cycle to Phase
recommendations.how: Slash Table and slash Draw io match board colors slash page for children then page options Publish Page Export PDF Word Markdown

key_findings: Self hosted Plane has no public Pages REST API
Page code lives only under apps api plane app session authed
X API Key page calls 404 issues 8986 7319 8598
Documented page endpoints are Plane Cloud only
Author the onboarding page in the web UI
Identifier max 12 force uppercased unique no specials
Use plain ASCP yielding items ASCP 1 to ASCP 243
Description short narrative goes on a Page
Page body HTML access lock icon parent FK nesting
PageLabel tags pages PageVersion gives history
Slash menu has 16 plus blocks Callout Table Draw io Math
Work item embed via at sign and create item from selection
Use Project Pages over Wiki for the single board
Lock Export PDF Word Markdown Publish a no login URL
Nested via slash page visibility not inherited
12 states Backlog Triaged Compiling Verified Approved Executing
then InVerification HumanReview Done HANDOFF Blocked Failed
Zero context leads mission first Truss Microsoft oneuptime
area: Plane Pages wiki and zero-context onboarding plus project identifier and description best practices for the self-hosted ASCP board
summary:
On self-hosted Plane, Pages are NOT in the public REST API (X-API-Key); page code lives only under the internal app session-authed API, so API-key calls 404 (open issues 8986, 7319, 8598), and the page endpoints on developers.plane.so describe Plane Cloud only. So author the ASCP onboarding page in the web UI. The identifier is capped at max_length 12, force-uppercased on save, unique per workspace, and rejects specials including hyphen and period (use ASCP, yielding ASCP-1 through ASCP-243); the description field is a short summary so the narrative belongs on a pinned Page. Pages are a Notion-style block editor (16-plus blocks, nested parent pages, HTML body, labels, version history, publish-to-web). Build one canonical ASCP Start Here Project Page from a fixed 7-section mission-first template (mission, 12-state legend, how to read the board, state machine, traceability, cycles/phases map, links) with a legend Table, a native draw.io state machine, at-mentioned live work items, and shallow nested children, then Lock and optionally Publish.</summary>
</invoke>

area: Industry enterprise PM (Linear/Jira/Shortcut) hierarchy, sprint/cycle structuring, WIP discipline, naming, compaction — applied to a Tier-1 Plane board for the ASCP project (243 work items, 12 states, 22 labels) mapping the .kiro Spec-to-Evidence platform (7 phases, 49 waves, 57 tasks)
summary: The Tier-1 pattern across Linear/Jira/Shortcut converges on a strict, shallow hierarchy (Epic -> Story -> Task -> Sub-task, max 4 levels), time-boxed cycles for *when* work happens, and a separate orthogonal grouping for *what* the work is. Plane maps cleanly: Epic (work-item type) = your 8 epics; sub-work-items via the Parent field give Story->Task->Subtask; Cycles = sprints/waves (the *time* axis); Modules = the 7 phases/feature-areas (the *structural* axis, and a work item can sit in a Feature module AND a Release module at once). Cycles and Modules are fully REST-driven (POST .../cycles/, POST .../modules/, plus .../cycles/{id}/issues/ and .../modules/{id}/issues/ to attach work items). Views and Pages are web-UI only — there is NO public REST endpoint for either (confirmed open GitHub issues #7319 pages, #8986 self-hosted pages, view-create absent from the API reference) — so the board's legibility layer (saved Kanban/spreadsheet views, grouping/sub-grouping, the zero-context onboarding page) must be configured manually in the UI and documented as a one-time setup, not provisioned by script. Key Tier-1 disciplines: outcome-driven epic names not 'Refactor X'; short scannable issue titles (Linear: 'write issues not user stories'); WIP limits per board column to surface bottlenecks; one active cycle at a time (parallel cycles only on Enterprise Grid) with explicit transfer of incomplete items; a small orthogonal label taxonomy using prefixes (type:, area:, phase:, risk:) rather than 22 flat labels; and routine triage to keep the backlog lean (archive/close stale, snooze deferred).
key_findings: HIERARCHY — keep it strict and shallow (max 4 levels). The universal Tier-1 rule is Epic -> Story -> Task -> Sub-task; adding more levels breaks dependency tracking, reporting, and portfolio rollups. Linear's stricter framing is Initiative -> Project -> Issue -> Sub-issue, with the decision rule: 'each level must genuinely contain multiple children — if it doesn't, the level is wrong.' Apply this to ASCP: 8 Epics (true containers), 49 EARS user stories as Stories, 186 tasks as Tasks, subtasks only where a task genuinely decomposes. Do NOT promote a single-child item up a level.

PLANE HIERARCHY MECHANICS — Epics are now a work-item TYPE, not a separate sidebar tab. Enabling Work Item Types (Project Settings -> Features) auto-creates two types: Task (level 0) and Epic (level 1). The dedicated Epics sidebar tab was removed; you now filter Work Items by Type = Epic. Children attach to an epic via the Parent field. Sub-work-items give the deeper Story/Task/Subtask nesting and CAN cross projects (a sub-work-item in Project A can parent to a work item in Project B). One work item belongs to at most one Epic.

CYCLES = the TIME axis (sprints/your 49 waves). By default only ONE cycle is active at a time and dates cannot overlap; parallel/overlapping cycles require Enterprise Grid (Project Settings -> Features -> Cycles toggle). A work item can be in only ONE cycle at a time. When a cycle's due date passes, you explicitly Transfer incomplete items to an active/upcoming cycle (UI transfer interface, or REST transfer-issues endpoint). Burn-down supports count-of-items OR point-estimates; leading=green (ahead), trailing=red (behind). Auto-scheduled cycles (Business plan) auto-number 'Sprint 1, 2...' with configurable duration, cooldown, start day, N pre-scheduled cycles, and auto-rollover of incomplete items. Workspace > Cycles shows active cycles across all projects.

MODULES = the STRUCTURAL axis (your 7 phases / feature-areas / 'discrete architecture pieces such as a microservice'). Crucially ORTHOGONAL to cycles: a work item can belong to a Feature module AND a Release module SIMULTANEOUSLY (multi-module assignment), whereas it can only be in one cycle. Modules have their own status lifecycle (backlog, planned, in-progress, paused, completed, cancelled), a lead, and members (members need not be assignees). This is the right home for the 7 Spec-to-Evidence Phases 0-6 — Phase = Module, Wave/Sprint = Cycle.

CYCLES + MODULES ARE FULLY REST-DRIVEN. Create cycle: POST /api/v1/workspaces/{slug}/projects/{pid}/cycles/ (required: name; optional: description, start_date, end_date, owned_by, timezone, external_id, external_source). Attach items: POST .../cycles/{cycle_id}/issues/. Transfer: POST .../cycles/{cycle_id}/transfer-issues/. Create module: POST .../projects/{pid}/modules/ (required: name; optional: description, start_date, target_date, status [backlog|planned|in-progress|paused|completed|cancelled], lead, members[], external_id, external_source). Attach items: POST .../modules/{module_id}/issues/. NOTE: the legacy /issues/ path segment is being deprecated in favor of /work-items/ (support ended 2026-03-31) — use external_id/external_source on every create to make the provisioning script idempotent and traceable back to .kiro IDs.

VIEWS and PAGES are WEB-UI ONLY — no public REST API (confirmed). The API reference exposes no view-create/update endpoints, and Pages are not reachable via /api/v1/ even on self-hosted (open issues makeplane/plane #7319 and #8986). Implication for a script-provisioned Tier-1 board: the data layer (epics, stories, tasks, cycles, modules, labels, states) is automatable; the LEGIBILITY layer (saved Views, the zero-context onboarding Page) must be built once by hand in the UI and captured in a board-navigation guide. Project Views support List/Board(Kanban)/Calendar/Table/Gantt with grouping by State/Priority/Label/Assignee/Cycle/Module and Board-layout sub-grouping (secondary axis). Workspace Views are spreadsheet-layout only and ship four non-deletable defaults (All Issues, Assigned to Me, Created by Me, Subscribed). Pro adds Lock, Publish (shareable link), private views, and CSV/XLSX/JSON export.

NAMING — outcome-driven, scannable, consistent. Epics: name by outcome not activity ('Enable Faster Checkout' not 'Refactor Backend'); an epic that equals a whole project is too big — split it. Cross-team legibility collapses when one team writes 'Login bug' and another 'AUTH-127 unable to sign in'; adopt one convention, e.g. '[Area] – [Outcome] – [Phase/Quarter]'. Issues/Tasks (Linear Method): 'write SHORT, simple titles that directly state the task,' scannable on a board; minimal description ('only as much as you need'); quote user/spec feedback verbatim rather than summarizing. Linear explicitly rejects ceremonial user-story prose ('cargo cult ritual') — but note ASCP's stories are EARS-format requirements, which is the opposite of bloat: keep them as precise testable shall-statements, not narrative stories.

WIP DISCIPLINE — make bottlenecks visible. Atlassian: WIP limit = max cards allowed in a column; set per-column AFTER observing average column occupancy for a few cycles, not a priori. When a column maxes out (e.g. In-Verification or Human-Review piling up), the team must SWARM to clear it before pulling new work in. For ASCP's 12 agent-lifecycle states, the highest-risk WIP columns to cap are Agent-Executing, In-Verification, and Human-Review — a backlog there means the agent loop is producing faster than verification/review can absorb. Plane surfaces this via Board-layout grouping by State; cap counts are a team policy enforced visually (Plane has no hard per-column WIP gate, so encode the limit in the view name/onboarding doc).

COMPACTION — orthogonal label dimensions with prefixes, not a flat pile. 22 flat labels become unreadable; Tier-1 teams use a small set of ORTHOGONAL dimensions and reserve structured fields for structured data. Rule of thumb: anything that is a stable functional area belongs in a Module (Plane's structured 'component' equivalent), anything time-bound belongs in a Cycle, anything a true type/state belongs in the work-item State or Priority — labels are only for the cross-cutting, flexible dimension that none of those capture. Use a prefix scheme so labels self-group in pickers: type:, area:, phase:, risk:, agent:. Plane labels are case-sensitive exact-match for filters. Audit the 22: collapse any that duplicate a state, a module, or a cycle.

BACKLOG HYGIENE / ANTI-CLUTTER is a recurring ritual, not a cleanup event. Linear: a 20-minute routine beats occasional big sweeps; archive anything untouched 30+ days that isn't critical; close or rewrite stale issues monthly; snooze (hide until a chosen time or new activity) deferred items; decline-with-comment rather than leaving zombies. Close epics the moment they complete to preserve reporting accuracy and avoid board clutter. For ASCP, your custom states already give a triage funnel (Backlog -> Agent-Triaged); enforce that nothing lingers in Backlog unscoped and that Done/Failed items are archived per cycle so burn-down stays accurate.

recommendations.title: Map the two axes explicitly: Phases -> Modules (structural), Waves/Sprints -> Cycles (temporal)
recommendations.detail: The single biggest legibility win is separating WHAT from WHEN. Make each of the 7 Spec-to-Evidence Phases (0-6) a Module, and each of the 49 dependency-graph waves (or a sprint-sized batch of waves) a Cycle. A task in Phase 3 that ships in Wave 27 then carries Module=Phase-3 AND Cycle=Wave-27 — and because Plane allows multi-module assignment, a task spanning a phase boundary or a release can also carry a Release module. This gives two independent, filterable rollups (phase progress vs sprint burn-down) without nesting them into one overloaded hierarchy.
recommendations.how: Create modules: POST /api/v1/workspaces/{slug}/projects/{pid}/modules/ with {"name":"Phase 3 — <outcome>","status":"planned","lead":"<uuid>","external_id":"phase-3","external_source":"kiro"} for each of the 7 phases. Create cycles: POST .../projects/{pid}/cycles/ with {"name":"Wave 27","start_date":"...","end_date":"...","external_id":"wave-27","external_source":"kiro"}. Attach a task to both: POST .../modules/{mid}/issues/ and POST .../cycles/{cid}/issues/ with the work-item id. Set external_id on every create so the script is idempotent and each Plane object traces back to a .kiro phase/wave id.

recommendations.title: Enforce the strict 4-level hierarchy via Work Item Types + Parent, and audit for single-child levels
recommendations.detail: Turn on Work Item Types so Epic (level 1) and Task (level 0) exist as real types, then build Epic -> Story -> Task -> Subtask through the Parent field and sub-work-items. Keep it to exactly these 4 levels. Apply Linear's decision rule during provisioning: if any of the 8 epics or 49 stories would contain only one child, flatten it — a one-child epic is mislabeled work. This keeps the tree shallow enough that dependency lines and burn-down stay readable at 243 items.
recommendations.how: Enable in Project Settings -> Features -> Work Item Types (auto-creates Task + Epic). In the API, set the parent relationship on each child work item's parent field; filter the board by Type = Epic to see the top layer. Validate before commit: for each parent, assert child_count >= 2 (or fold the lone child up a level). Use sub-work-items (cross-project capable) for the Task -> Subtask leaf level.

recommendations.title: Cap WIP on the three risk columns and encode the limits where the UI will show them
recommendations.detail: Plane has no hard per-column WIP enforcement, so make the limit visible instead. The columns that will silently back up in an agentic loop are Agent-Executing, In-Verification, and Human-Review — if the agent produces faster than verification/review drains, work piles invisibly. Observe each column's average occupancy for ~2 cycles, then set a cap and bake it into the board's policy so the team swarms to clear a maxed column before pulling new work.
recommendations.how: Build a saved Board (Kanban) view grouped by State (UI: project -> Views -> Add view -> layout Board -> group by State). Name the columns/view to carry the cap, e.g. a view titled 'Execution Board (WIP: Executing<=5, In-Verification<=3, Human-Review<=2)'. Document the swarm rule in the onboarding Page. Re-derive caps from Workspace > Cycles burn-down after two sprints. (Views are UI-only — no API — so this is a documented one-time setup.)

recommendations.title: Run one active cycle at a time with an explicit end-of-sprint transfer ritual
recommendations.detail: Unless you are on Enterprise Grid, only one cycle can be active and cycle dates cannot overlap — a work item lives in exactly one cycle. So model the 49 waves as a SEQUENCE of cycles (or sprint-batched cycles), not 49 overlapping ones, and at each sprint boundary explicitly transfer unfinished items forward so burn-down and velocity stay accurate. This is also how you avoid the classic 'ghost cycle' where completed-cycle metrics drift.
recommendations.how: At sprint end: POST /api/v1/workspaces/{slug}/projects/{pid}/cycles/{cycle_id}/transfer-issues/ to move incomplete items to the next cycle. If you want zero manual rollover, upgrade to the Business plan and enable auto-scheduled cycles (Project Settings -> Features -> Cycles) with auto-rollover, fixed duration, cooldown, and N pre-scheduled cycles. View cross-project sprint status at Workspace > Cycles. Choose burn-down by point-estimates if estimates are enabled, else by item count.

recommendations.title: Collapse the 22 flat labels into a prefixed, orthogonal taxonomy and move structured dimensions off labels
recommendations.detail: 22 flat labels are a clutter source and likely duplicate data that belongs in structured fields. Reserve Modules for stable functional/area grouping, Cycles for time, and State/Priority for lifecycle — then labels carry only the genuinely cross-cutting flexible dimension. Use a prefix scheme so labels self-group in the picker and read cleanly on cards: type:, area:, phase:, risk:, agent:. Audit each of the 22 and delete any that restate a state, module, or cycle.
recommendations.how: For each label, ask: is this a phase (-> Module), a sprint (-> Cycle), a lifecycle stage (-> one of the 12 States), or a priority (-> Priority field)? If yes, retire the label and migrate the dimension. Keep the rest as prefixed labels (e.g. risk:high, area:orchestration, agent:executor). Labels are case-sensitive exact-match in filters — pick one casing convention. Aim to land well under 22; a focused core beats full coverage.

recommendations.title: Build the zero-context onboarding layer as UI Views + one Pages doc, and treat it as un-scriptable
recommendations.detail: Because Views and Pages have NO public REST API (open issues #7319/#8986 confirm Pages are UI-only even self-hosted, and view-create is absent from the API reference), the board's human-facing legibility layer cannot be provisioned by the same script that creates the data. Build it once by hand and document it so it survives re-creation: a small set of saved Views (Epic roadmap, Current sprint board, Phase rollup, My work, Blocked/Failed triage) plus one Pages doc that explains the 12 states, the 22->prefixed labels, the Phase=Module / Wave=Cycle model, and the WIP swarm rule.
recommendations.how: Project -> Views -> Add view for each saved lens (set layout + group-by + filters, then Save). Recommended set: (1) 'Epic Roadmap' Gantt grouped by Epic; (2) 'Current Sprint' Board grouped by State with WIP caps in the title; (3) 'Phase Rollup' Table grouped by Module; (4) 'Blocked/Failed Triage' filtered State in {HANDOFF, Blocked, Failed}; (5) Workspace view 'Active Cycles'. Create one Page (press the Pages create action in the UI) as the onboarding README. Capture all of this in the board-navigation guide since it is not reproducible via API — note Lock/Publish (Pro) if you want read-only shareable versions.

recommendations.title: Adopt outcome-driven names and short scannable titles; keep EARS stories as precise shall-statements
recommendations.detail: Name the 8 epics by outcome ('Enable verifiable agent execution') not by activity ('Build orchestrator'). Give the 186 tasks short, direct titles that state the action and read at a glance on a board (Linear's 'write issues not user stories'). Linear's anti-user-story stance is about killing ceremonial narrative prose — it does NOT mean loosen your EARS requirements; keep the 49 stories as tight, testable 'WHEN <trigger> the system SHALL <response>' statements, which are the rigorous opposite of bloat.
recommendations.how: Epic title pattern: '[Capability/Outcome]'. Task title pattern: imperative + object ('Add wave-dependency resolver', not 'Wave dependency resolution work'). Keep descriptions minimal and link the .kiro spec section rather than restating it. For cross-team consistency adopt one scheme, e.g. epic '[Area] – [Outcome]', and apply it uniformly so the board reads as one system.

recommendations.title: Institutionalize a recurring triage/hygiene ritual to keep the 243-item board lean
recommendations.detail: Boards rot without a cadence. Adopt Linear's discipline: a short recurring triage (≈20 min) beats sporadic cleanups. Archive items untouched 30+ days that aren't critical, close or rewrite stale items monthly, snooze deferred work, decline-with-comment instead of leaving zombies, and CLOSE each epic and archive each cycle's Done/Failed items the moment they complete so burn-down and phase rollups stay accurate.
recommendations.how: Use the Backlog -> Agent-Triaged states as the intake funnel: nothing stays in Backlog unscoped. Per sprint close: archive completed cycles (POST .../cycles/{id}/archive/) and archive Done items so the active board only shows live work. Build the 'Blocked/Failed Triage' view (State in {HANDOFF, Blocked, Failed}) as the standing triage queue. Schedule the 20-minute review at each cycle boundary and document it in the onboarding Page so it survives team changes.

area: Traceability, observability, and zero-context onboarding for a Tier-1 agentic-SDLC board in Plane (ASCP: 8 epics / 49 EARS stories / 186 tasks; .kiro Spec-to-Evidence 7 phases / 49 waves / 57 tasks)
summary: Plane's REST API exposes exactly the primitives needed for enterprise-grade requirement-to-work-item-to-evidence traceability, and Plane's web-UI adds the observability and navigation layer. Three findings dominate. (1) TRACEABILITY: Plane now supports user-defined custom relations (defaults Relates to / Duplicate / Implements + dependency types Blocked by / Blocking / Starts before, with admin-created customs like Tests, Validates, Mitigates, Part of, Supersedes) AND custom properties of 10 types (TEXT, DATETIME, DECIMAL, BOOLEAN, OPTION, RELATION[ISSUE|USER], URL, EMAIL, FILE, FORMULA) per work-item type — this is the spine for EARS-ID <-> wave <-> phase <-> evidence linkage. Every external artifact (PR, CI run, spec file, test report) attaches via the Work Item Links API and the external_id/external_source fields that exist on work items, properties, property-values, and link options for idempotent sync. (2) OBSERVABILITY: workspace-scoped custom Dashboards (web-UI only, read-only) with 7 chart types answer 'for these items, grouped by X, what is metric Y' where X can be a CUSTOM property and Y includes blocked-item and pending counts; Cycles give burn-down/build-up Progress Charts with auto-roll and velocity; the immutable Work Item Activity feed is the audit trail every agentic-SDLC governance framework demands. (3) ONBOARDING: Pages/Wiki ARE API-creatable (unlike Views/Dashboards which are web-UI only), support nested hierarchy and embedded work-items/cycles, giving a 'Start Here' single-source-of-truth that sits next to the work. The board structure that maps the spec: Epics (native) -> EARS Stories (work items, type=Story) -> Tasks/Subtasks (parent hierarchy); Phases 0-6 as Modules; Waves as Cycles; the 12 agent-lifecycle states as the project state set; 22 labels for cross-cutting concerns. KEY CONSTRAINT: Views, Dashboards, and Wiki *rendering* are web-UI only — build them once by hand; everything else (items, relations, properties, property values, links, pages, cycles, modules, states, labels) is fully API-drivable for reproducible provisioning.
key_findings: TRACEABILITY SPINE - Custom Relations are user-definable (NEW). Plane ships defaults: 'Relates to', 'Duplicate', 'Implements' plus dependency types 'Blocked by', 'Blocking', 'Starts before'. Workspace admins can create ADDITIONAL custom relation types; Plane documents teams using 'Tests', 'Validates', 'Mitigates', 'Replaces', 'Supersedes', 'Part of'. This is the exact vocabulary for an agentic SDLC: an EARS Story --Implements--> Epic, a Task --Tests--> Story, a verification item --Validates--> Plan. Source: plane.so/blog/new-work-item-detail-view-custom-relations-and-more.

TRACEABILITY SPINE - Custom Properties (10 types) extend each work-item TYPE. POST /api/v1/workspaces/{slug}/projects/{project_id}/work-item-types/{type_id}/work-item-properties/ with property_type in {TEXT, DATETIME, DECIMAL, BOOLEAN, OPTION, RELATION, URL, EMAIL, FILE, FORMULA} and relation_type in {ISSUE, USER}. Body fields: display_name, name, is_required, is_multi, is_active, default_value[], settings{}, validation_rules{}, options[] (each: name/description/is_default/external_id/external_source), formula_config. This is how you bolt EARS-ID, Phase#, Wave#, Spec-file-path, Acceptance-criteria-status, Evidence-URL, and Agent-run-ID onto every item as FIRST-CLASS FILTERABLE fields, not free text.

TRACEABILITY - Property VALUES are upserted per item: POST /api/v1/workspaces/{slug}/projects/{project_id}/work-items/{work_item_id}/work-item-properties/{property_id}/values/ body {"value": <typed>, "external_id": "...", "external_source": "..."}. It is an UPSERT (one value per item/property), value typing: string for text/url/email, UUID(s) for relation/option, YYYY-MM-DD for date, number for decimal, bool for boolean. Scope required: projects.work_item_property_values:write.

TRACEABILITY - Evidence linkage via Work Item Links API: POST .../work-items/{work_item_id}/links/ body {"url":"...","title":"...","metadata":{}}. Attach GitHub PRs, CI run URLs, test reports, .kiro spec files. Full CRUD (Add/List/Get/Update/Delete). For programmatic idempotency, external_id + external_source appear on work items, custom properties, property options, AND property values — set external_source='kiro' / external_id=<EARS-ID> so re-runs reconcile instead of duplicate.

HIERARCHY - 4-level tree is native: Epics (dedicated epic type, can span projects) -> Work Items as Stories (set type_id) -> Tasks via parent field -> Subtasks via parent field. Create: POST .../work-items/ with name, type_id, parent, state, labels[], assignees[], priority{urgent|high|medium|low|none}, start_date, target_date, external_id, external_source. The redesigned detail view shows parent as a pill next to the work-item ID and a 'Copy branch name' button generating e.g. 'sarah/ASCP-606' — direct code<->item traceability.

PLANNING AXES - Cycles = time-boxed sprints/waves (REST): POST .../cycles/ (name,start_date,end_date,owned_by,timezone); attach items POST .../cycles/{cycle_id}/cycle-issues/. Modules = feature/phase groupings (REST): POST .../modules/ (name,lead,members[],start_date,target_date,status{backlog|planned|in-progress|paused|completed|cancelled}); attach via .../modules/{module_id}/module-issues/. RECOMMENDED MAPPING: 49 dependency-graph Waves -> Cycles (gives per-wave burn-down + velocity); 7 Phases (0-6) -> Modules (gives phase-level rollup independent of time). An item lives in exactly one cycle but can map a phase via module — both axes simultaneously.

OBSERVABILITY - Dashboards are WORKSPACE-scoped, READ-ONLY, WEB-UI ONLY (no public API — build once by hand). 7 chart types: Bar (basic/stacked/grouped), Line (basic/multi), Area (basic/stacked/comparison), Pie/Donut (donut adds progress), Number, Table. Per-widget config = X-axis (group by ANY property incl. CUSTOM, state, priority, assignee, label, cycle, module), Y-axis metric (work-item count, estimate points, pending count, COMPLETED count, BLOCKED count, due-date count), optional secondary group-by, two-tier filters (dashboard-level + widget-level). This is how 'work items grouped by agent-lifecycle-state, counting blocked' becomes a live tile.

OBSERVABILITY - Progress Charts on Cycles give Burn-down OR Build-up, ideal-vs-actual pace, auto-roll of incomplete items to next cycle, and cross-iteration velocity comparison — the per-wave delivery signal. Complements Dashboards (cross-cutting) with time-boxed flow.

OBSERVABILITY / AUDIT TRAIL - Work Item Activity is an immutable system-generated feed capturing state changes, assignee/priority/label changes, cycle/module assignment, date/estimate changes, CUSTOM PROPERTY VALUE changes, and RELATION changes. GET .../issue-activity/. Plus Comments (comment_html) for agent run-logs. Industry agentic-SDLC governance (EU AI Act Art. 14, HITL frameworks) requires exactly this: every agent decision traceable to a rule/policy/diff with full provenance ('not just what happened, but why').

ONBOARDING - Pages/Wiki ARE API-creatable (key differentiator from Views/Dashboards). Project pages: POST /api/workspaces/{slug}/projects/{project_id}/pages/ ; Workspace wiki: POST /api/workspaces/{slug}/pages/ ; body {name, description_html}. Pages support NESTED hierarchy and EMBED work items, cycles, and project updates via the '/' command (16 block types). Pages live inside projects, Wikis span them — so a 'Start Here' onboarding page sits next to the work it describes. Notion/Confluence import preserves hierarchy.

ONBOARDING - Views are saved layout+filter+grouping+sort bookmarks, WEB-UI ONLY (no API). Project Views (all layouts) + Workspace Views (spreadsheet; 4 non-deletable defaults: All Issues, Assigned to Me, Created by Me, Subscribed) + Teamspace Views. Layouts: List, Board (Kanban, supports sub-group/swimlanes), Table (spreadsheet bulk-edit), Calendar, Timeline (Gantt with dependency lines). Views are the 'get up to speed instantly' surface — e.g. 'All blockers this wave', 'Awaiting Human-Review', 'Failed runs'.

ZERO-CONTEXT INDUSTRY PATTERN - One home base / single source of truth: pick ONE platform, define what MUST live there vs elsewhere, intuitive structure with consistent naming, a literal 'Start Here' path for newcomers, templates for updates/decision-logs/checklists, and a named OWNER per space. An 'onboarding issue/page' prevents fragmented Slack/email/Doc sprawl. New joiner should find overview + decision history + current tasks in ONE place without asking anyone.

AUTH / OPS - Base https://api.plane.so/api/v1/, header X-API-Key: <PAT> (or Authorization: Bearer for OAuth). Cursor pagination (per_page, cursor; returns next_cursor/prev_cursor/total_results). Rate limit 60 req/min (watch X-RateLimit-Remaining) — batch provisioning of 243 items must throttle/backoff. fields= and expand= for selective/nested retrieval. Self-host parity is full (Docker/K8s/air-gapped). NOTE: docs show both /issues/ and /work-items/ path forms; /work-items/ is the current canonical naming — standardize on it.

recommendations.title: Define the traceability vocabulary as custom relation types + an EARS-ID custom property
recommendations.detail: The single highest-leverage Tier-1 move. Create workspace custom relations 'Implements', 'Verifies/Tests', 'Validates', 'Traces-to' on top of the built-in Blocked by/Blocking/Relates to. Then make EARS-ID a first-class filterable field, not text buried in titles. Result: any item answers 'which requirement, which phase, which wave, what evidence' by structured query — the auditable RTM the whole board hinges on.
recommendations.how: Relations: Workspace Settings -> create custom relation types (web UI; admin only). Per work-item-type custom properties via API: POST /api/v1/workspaces/{slug}/projects/{pid}/work-item-types/{type_id}/work-item-properties/ -> create (a) 'EARS-ID' property_type=TEXT is_required=true; (b) 'Phase' property_type=OPTION with options[] = Phase 0..6 (is_default on Phase 0); (c) 'Wave' property_type=DECIMAL; (d) 'Spec-File' property_type=URL; (e) 'Evidence' property_type=URL is_multi=true; (f) 'Acceptance-Status' property_type=OPTION options=[Not-Started,Drafted,Verified,Signed-off]. Set values: POST .../work-items/{wid}/work-item-properties/{prop_id}/values/ {"value":"US-12","external_source":"kiro","external_id":"US-12"}. Wire each Story --Implements--> its Epic and each Task --Verifies--> its Story using the relation UI or the relation endpoints.

recommendations.title: Provision the full hierarchy and both planning axes idempotently via REST with external_id
recommendations.detail: Make the 243 work items reproducible from the .kiro spec. Map Epics->native epics, 49 EARS Stories->Story-typed work items, 186 Tasks->parent-linked children. Put the 7 Phases on Modules and the 49 Waves on Cycles so you get BOTH a phase rollup and a per-wave burn-down. Tag external_id/external_source on everything so re-running the provisioner reconciles instead of duplicating.
recommendations.how: Create items: POST /api/v1/workspaces/{slug}/projects/{pid}/work-items/ {name, type_id:<Story|Task>, parent:<uuid|null>, state:<uuid>, labels:[...], priority, start_date, target_date, external_id:'US-12', external_source:'kiro'}. Phases: POST .../modules/ then POST .../modules/{mid}/module-issues/. Waves: POST .../cycles/ (name,start_date,end_date,timezone) then POST .../cycles/{cid}/cycle-issues/. Respect the 60 req/min limit: batch with backoff on X-RateLimit-Remaining. Standardize on the /work-items/ path form. Use expand=labels,assignees,state to verify after write.

recommendations.title: Attach evidence as structured Links and treat the Activity feed as the audit trail
recommendations.detail: Tier-1/AI-Labs grade means every claim is backed by an artifact and every agent action is provenance-logged. Link each item to its PR, CI run, test report, and spec section; never paste raw URLs into descriptions. The immutable Work Item Activity feed plus comment run-logs is your EU-AI-Act-Article-14-style 'reasoning trace' for human-in-the-loop review.
recommendations.how: Evidence: POST /api/v1/workspaces/{slug}/projects/{pid}/work-items/{wid}/links/ {"url":"https://github.com/.../pull/42","title":"PR #42 implements US-12","metadata":{}}. Agent run-log: POST a comment (comment_html) per agent action summarizing decision + diff + policy applied. Read provenance: GET .../issue-activity/ (captures state, relation, and custom-property-value changes automatically). Use the detail view's 'Copy branch name' (e.g. ascp/ASCP-606) so commits/PRs carry the item ID for two-way code traceability.

recommendations.title: Build the observability layer: one workspace Dashboard + per-Cycle Progress Charts
recommendations.detail: Dashboards are web-UI only and read-only, so build the canonical 'ASCP Command Center' once by hand. Drive widgets off the agent-lifecycle states and custom properties for at-a-glance status legibility. Pair with Cycle burn-down for per-wave flow. This is the observability surface that makes the board legible without reading 243 items.
recommendations.how: Workspace -> Dashboards -> New (workspace-scoped, set Public). Add widgets: (1) Bar grouped-by State (the 12 agent states: Backlog/Agent-Triaged/Spec-Compiling/Spec-Verified/Plan-Approved/Agent-Executing/In-Verification/Human-Review/Done + HANDOFF/Blocked/Failed), metric=count; (2) Number widget metric=BLOCKED count filtered to Blocked+Failed+HANDOFF; (3) Donut grouped-by Phase (custom OPTION property); (4) Bar grouped-by custom 'Acceptance-Status'; (5) Table grouped-by Wave. Per wave: open the Cycle -> Progress Charts -> Burn-down, enable auto-roll and velocity comparison.

recommendations.title: Stand up a 'Start Here' Wiki page + a saved-Views navigation set for zero-context onboarding
recommendations.detail: A newcomer (human or agent) should reach full context in one click. Pages/Wiki are API-creatable, so generate the onboarding page from the spec; Views are web-UI only, so curate the canonical navigation set by hand. Embed live work items into the page so the onboarding doc never goes stale.
recommendations.how: Page: POST /api/workspaces/{slug}/projects/{pid}/pages/ {name:'ASCP — Start Here', description_html:'<the spec map: 8 epics -> 49 stories -> 186 tasks, phase/wave legend, state-machine legend, label glossary, links to dashboards>'}. In-UI, use '/' to embed the Command Center cycle and key epics. Create Views (web UI): 'Awaiting Human-Review' (filter state=Human-Review), 'Active Wave — Blockers' (state in Blocked/Failed/HANDOFF), 'Spec Phase Board' (Board layout grouped-by Phase custom property), 'My Agent Queue' (Board grouped-by state). Name one owner for the Wiki space per the single-source-of-truth pattern.

recommendations.title: Add a label taxonomy and a state-machine legend so the 22 labels and 12 states are self-documenting
recommendations.detail: 22 labels and a 12-state custom workflow are only assets if their meaning is obvious. Group labels by prefix namespace and document the state transitions (esp. the HANDOFF/Blocked/Failed exception states and the fixed infinite-block defect) so status is legible at a glance — a core zero-context requirement.
recommendations.how: Labels via API: POST /api/v1/workspaces/{slug}/projects/{pid}/labels/ using consistent prefixes (e.g. area:*, risk:*, gate:*, agent:*) so the Board's group-by-label and Table filters read cleanly. States via API: POST .../states/ for the 12 lifecycle states with explicit group mapping (backlog/unstarted/started/completed/cancelled) and color coding; document the legal transitions and the HANDOFF semantics on the Start-Here page. Render a state legend + label glossary as a nested Wiki sub-page embedded in onboarding.
